345.reason: Error: When encountering a single '&', the code incorrectly adds an AND token with value "&" before adding the error. According to the problem description, a single '&' should be treated as an error (type 'a') and should not produce a valid token. The token should not be added.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.25 , reason:  Error: When encountering a single '&', the code incorrectly adds an AND token with value "&" before adding the error. According to the problem description, a single '&' should be treated as an error (type 'a') and should not produce a valid token. The token should not be added.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.25 , reason:  Error: When a single '&' is encountered, the token type should be something else (e.g., a separate token type for '&' alone), but here it incorrectly uses AND. Also, after adding the error, the code still adds an AND token for "&&" on the next line, which is wrong because the condition already determined it's not '&&'.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Incorrectly adds AND token for single '&' before checking validity; should not add any AND token here]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Incorrectly adds AND token for single '&'; should not add any valid token for single '&' as it's an error]

349.reason: [This line unconditionally adds "&&" token even when ch != '&', causing two tokens: one "&" and one "&&". It should be inside an else block or only added when ch == '&'.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.25 , reason:  Error: This line is executed unconditionally after the if-else block, meaning that even when a single '&' is detected (and an error is added), it still adds an AND token with value "&&". This is incorrect because the single '&' should not produce any token, and the '&&' token should only be added when two '&' are found.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.25 , reason:  Error: This line is executed unconditionally after the if-else block, meaning even when a single '&' is found (and an error is added), it still adds a token for "&&". This should be inside an else branch.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  Error: This token is added unconditionally even when a single '&' is detected and an error is added. Should be inside an else block.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  Missing else: This line executes unconditionally, causing a double token to be added even when an error is detected.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  This line executes unconditionally. It should be wrapped in an else block to prevent adding the "&&" token when a single "&" error is detected.
